name: Systems Engineering V-Model
summary: A development model in which requirements are decomposed on the left side of the V and verified through integration and test on the right side.
whenToUseIt: Use it for complex aerospace systems where subsystems must be designed separately yet still prove they meet the top-level mission need when integrated.
steps: Define stakeholder needs and top-level system requirements.
Decompose the system into subsystems and components.
Allocate requirements and interfaces down the architecture.
Build and verify components and subsystems against those allocations.
Integrate upward until the complete system is validated against the original need.
example: A UAV system may split into airframe, propulsion, avionics, ground station, and autonomy functions. The V-model prevents teams from building each part in isolation without a matching verification plan.
